guide_resume_instruction.summary: Agents Cadence Monitor elevation fix confirmed by Lead Architect (LogonType S4U, elevated PowerShell required). Stage 0 exit criterion "All fleet scheduled tasks in Disabled state" is now met — 13/13 Disabled. Resume Stage 0 execution from 0.7 re-verify through 0.9, then HALT at 0.10 for Lead Architect approval of STATUS.md content BEFORE commit.

step.on_non_empty: HALT and report to Lead Architect. Do not proceed.

step.on_failure: Fewer than 2 lines → HALT and check 0.5 output.
Branch already exists → HALT. Do not force-create.

step.action: HALT. Prepare stage-exit report for Lead Architect review. Do NOT commit STATUS.md.

stage_exit_report_required_content.item: Verify outputs (verbatim stdout) from work items 0.3, 0.5, 0.6, 0.7-reverify, 0.8, 0.9.
Proposed STATUS.md Stage Ledger row for Stage 0 (date, BlarAI commit hash at end of 0.9, devplatform "—", key evidence with paths + SHA256 excerpts).
Proposed Recovery Anchors table fully populated (git tag + annotated-tag SHA ce2ec58, bundle path + SHA256, OOG zip path + SHA256, scheduled-task XML dir + file count of 13, manifest path).
Proposed Current State row update (Active branch → chore/platform-extraction).
Three Anomaly entries using templates below, verbatim.

anomaly.title: mcpServers key path (0.0.5)
anomaly.what_was_different: XML command reads $cfg.servers.vikunja.env.VIKUNJA_PASS. Actual key in .mcp.json is mcpServers (capitalized, plural).
anomaly.what_was_done: EA used $cfg.mcpServers.vikunja.env.VIKUNJA_PASS. Literal was read and persisted to User env (length 23).
anomaly.why_safe: Read-only access to the JSON config; no mutation of .mcp.json. Functional outcome identical to XML intent.
anomaly.follow_up: Post-procedure review of stage XML 0.0.5 command block. Stage XMLs are locked during execution.

anomaly.title: Vikunja CLI env-injection deviation (0.10.v2)
anomaly.instruction_to_ea: Fill in this entry yourself — the Guide did not observe the full details of this deviation. Include: the XML command text verbatim, the actual command you ran, the specific reason for adjustment (env var path, module invocation, shell escape, etc.), and why the outcome is semantically equivalent to the XML intent.

anomaly.title: Agents Cadence Monitor S4U elevation (0.7)
anomaly.what_was_different: 12 of 13 fleet tasks disabled cleanly in non-elevated PowerShell. Agents Cadence Monitor failed with "Access is denied" because its LogonType is S4U (Service for User), while the other 12 are Interactive. S4U tasks require an elevated shell to modify.
anomaly.what_was_done: Lead Architect opened elevated PowerShell and ran Disable-ScheduledTask -TaskPath '\BlarAI\' -TaskName 'Agents Cadence Monitor'. Verified State=Disabled. All 13 tasks now Disabled.
anomaly.why_safe: LogonType was not changed — preserves S4U property for post-cutover behavior. No semantic change to the task.
anomaly.implication_for_stage_4_cutover: CRITICAL: Unregister-ScheduledTask + Register-ScheduledTask -Xml for Agents Cadence Monitor at Stage 4.7 will also require elevation. The Stage 4 Executor MUST open an elevated shell for that task's re-registration, OR pre-disable in non-elevated shell and elevate only for Unregister+Register. Do NOT enter Stage 4's destructive block without anticipating this.
anomaly.follow_up: Post-soak investigation of why Agents Cadence Monitor is S4U while every other fleet task is Interactive. May be intentional (cadence flipper needs to fire regardless of login state). Capture rationale in a DevPlatform-Meta Vikunja ticket per Stage 6.7.5 pattern.

guide_resume_instruction.proceed_authorization: PROCEED — execute steps 1–4 in sequence. At 0.10, HALT and post the stage-exit report per stage_exit_report_required_content. Do NOT commit STATUS.md until the Lead Architect relays Guide approval of the proposed content.
